name: Nocturne
description: Lyrical night piece in ternary form with ornamental melody over arpeggiated accompaniment

sections.name: Theme A
sections.description: Main lyrical theme, cantabile melody in the right hand over broken chord accompaniment
sections.melody_character: Singing, legato line with long notes on strong beats and ornamental turns, trills, grace notes on weak beats. Phrases typically 4 bars long with clear arch-shaped contour (ascending to climax, then descending to rest).
sections.accompaniment_character: Steady broken chord pattern (arpeggiated left hand), typically root-fifth-octave-fifth or similar nocturne pattern. Must be rhythmically regular to contrast with the free melody.
sections.harmonic_rhythm: One chord per bar, occasionally two in cadential bars
sections.ends_with: Half cadence or imperfect authentic cadence (creates expectation)

sections.name: Theme B (Contrasting)
sections.description: Contrasting middle section with increased intensity and different character
sections.key_relation: relative major (if tonic is minor), subdominant, or mediant
sections.melody_character: More agitated, faster note values, wider intervals. Can be more dramatic or passionate. Should contrast clearly with Theme A but share some motivic connection (interval or rhythm cell).
sections.accompaniment_character: Can shift to more active patterns: thicker chords, tremolo, or faster arpeggiation. May include dialogue between hands.
sections.harmonic_rhythm: Faster harmonic rhythm (2 chords per bar), more chromaticism, use of diminished and augmented chords
sections.ends_with: Dominant preparation (V or V7 of original key) to set up return

sections.name: Theme A' (Reprise)
sections.description: Return of Theme A with ornamental variation - the melody should be recognizably the same but embellished
sections.melody_character: CRITICAL: Must use the SAME melodic outline as Theme A but with added ornaments: chromatic passing tones, turns, trills, extended runs, cadenza-like flourishes. The listener must recognize Theme A underneath the decoration.
sections.accompaniment_character: Same pattern as Theme A but can be slightly fuller or with added inner voices
sections.harmonic_rhythm: Same as Theme A, possibly with richer substitutions
sections.ends_with: Perfect authentic cadence (V-I with melody arriving on tonic)

sections.name: Coda
sections.description: Brief closing passage that dissolves into silence
sections.melody_character: Fragments of Theme A, slowing down (written-out ritardando with longer note values). Final note should be a long tonic note.
sections.accompaniment_character: Thinning texture, wider spacing, final tonic chord
sections.harmonic_rhythm: Slow: plagal cadence (IV-I) or simple tonic pedal

composition_rules: Theme A' must reuse the melodic contour of Theme A with added ornamentation
Accompaniment pattern should remain consistent within sections for unity
Bass should move primarily by step or by fourth/fifth
Melody should occupy a higher register than accompaniment with clear separation
Use pedal markings (long bass notes) for sustained harmonic support
Chromaticism increases in Theme B and decreases in the Coda
